commit.message:
feat: type coercion, closure scoping, class inheritance conformance (#450)

* fix: use string_to_number in abstract_eq for spec-compliant String→Number coercion

The abstract_eq function in the interpreter used Rust's f64::parse() for
String→Number coercion, which does not follow ECMAScript semantics:
- Empty string "" parsed to NaN instead of 0
- Hex/octal/binary literals ("0x1A", "0o17", "0b101") were not handled

This caused incorrect results for:
- "" == false  (should be true: "" → 0, false → 0)
- [] == false  (should be true: [] → "" → 0, false → 0)
- [] == ![]    (should be true: ![] → false, then same as above)

Fix: make string_to_number pub(crate) and use it in abstract_eq instead of
the raw Rust parse, matching the is_loosely_equal_inner implementation in
value.rs which already used string_to_number correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix closure variable capture through scope chain

- Add closure_context field to BytecodeArray to store the captured
  enclosing context when CreateClosure creates a function closure.

- CreateClosure now captures the current frame context so inner
  functions can walk the scope chain to reach outer variables.

- All call dispatch sites (CallAnyReceiver, CallUndefinedReceiver0/1/2,
  CallProperty, CallWithSpread, Construct, ConstructWithSpread,
  ConstructForwardAllArgs, eval fallback, dispatch_call,
  dispatch_call_property) restore the closure context on the callee
  frame via restore_closure_context().

- TailCall handler restores closure context instead of setting it to
  None.

- Generator run_generator_step restores closure context so generator
  closures can access outer scope variables.

- LdaLookupContextSlot and LdaLookupContextSlotInsideTypeof now use
  the slot_idx and depth operands to read from the context chain (fast
  path) before falling back to global name-based lookup (slow path).
  Previously they ignored slot/depth and only did global lookup.

- Remove incorrect callee_frame.context = Some(this_val) assignments
  in CallProperty / Construct / ConstructWithSpread handlers that
  were overwriting the scope chain context with the receiver object.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix class inheritance conformance: construction, super(), new.target, fields

- handle_construct (Function): Bind 'this' in global env so constructor
  body can access it via LdaGlobal.
- handle_construct (PlainObject): Handle JsValue::Function in __call__
  for class constructors created by CreateClass. Create this object,
  wire prototype chain, set new_target, run field initializer.
- handle_construct_with_spread: Same PlainObject fix.
- handle_construct_forward_all_args: Add proper [[Construct]] semantics
  (create this, set new_target, handle PlainObject class ctors).
- handle_call_any_receiver (PlainObject): Handle Function in __call__
  so super() works in derived constructors.
- Parser: Handle TokenKind::Super as identifier 'super' in primary
  expressions so super() and super.method() parse correctly.
- Add call_plain_object_function helper for super() calls.
- Add construct_class_from_plain_object shared helper.
- Add 7 end-to-end class conformance tests covering: construction with
  this binding, static methods, static fields, instance fields,
  extends prototype chain, super() in constructors, new.target.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix/slm entity resolution 1338 (#1360)

* fix: improve SLM entity resolution, name search, and output formatting

The admin chat / SLM was effectively useless for natural language queries:
- 'show customer X' failed because 'customer' didn't match slug 'customers'
- Name-based lookups were impossible — only numeric IDs accepted
- Record output was just ToString() with no field data

Changes:
- Add fuzzy entity resolution (singular/plural, prefix, contains matching)
  in both AdminToolCatalogue (Intelligence) and QueryTools (AI)
- Add 'show-entity' intent + handler that loads by ID or searches by name
- Add SearchByName AI tool for the Copilot/IChatClient path
- Extract search text from natural language queries (everything after
  the entity name becomes a 'query' parameter)
- Format record output with actual field values instead of ToString()
- Improve help text to document new capabilities

Fixes #1338

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: expand SLM vocabulary pruner with full metadata graph

VocabularyPruner.FromDataScaffold() now captures the complete semantic
graph available to users:

- Entity names, slugs, and singular forms
- All field names, labels, types, and field groups
- Lookup/FK relationships (target slug + display field)
- Related document references and child entity slugs
- Remote command/action names and labels (from DataScaffold + RuntimeEntityRegistry)
- Permission tokens and nav groups
- Query operator vocabulary (contains, equals, greater than, etc.)
- Action verbs (create, update, delete, execute, invoke, etc.)
- Temporal/filter terms (first, last, latest, before, after, etc.)

SemanticPruner.GetDomainCorpus() now dynamically generates calibration
test cases from actual registered entity metadata — queries, field
filters on indexed fields, and action invocations — ensuring the
pruning accuracy gate reflects the real domain rather than hard-coded
example entities.

commit.message:
Align "Unhealthy" stat card color in Fleet to error.main (#2211)

`FleetOverviewTab` rendered its "Unhealthy" stat card in `warning.main`
(yellow), while `InventoryHealthSummaryCards` (Indices/Data Streams)
used `error.main` (red) for the same label — inconsistent severity
signal across pages.

- Changed `FleetOverviewTab.tsx` "Unhealthy" `FleetStatCard` color from
`"warning.main"` to `"error.main"`

```diff
 <FleetStatCard
   title="Unhealthy"
   value={serverStatus.unhealthy}
-  color="warning.main"
+  color="error.main"
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[ux-design-patrol] InventoryHealthSummaryCards maps
"Unhealthy" to error.main (red) while Fleet maps it to warning.main
(yellow)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Finding
> 
> Commit `d41133f2` introduced `InventoryHealthSummaryCards.tsx`, shared
by the Indices and Data Streams pages. Its "Unhealthy" stat card renders
the count in `error.main` (red). The pre-existing Fleet overview
(`FleetOverviewTab.tsx`) renders its "Unhealthy" stat card in
`warning.main` (yellow/orange). Users who switch between pages see the
same word "Unhealthy" convey two different severity signals through
color alone.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Evidence
> 
> **New pattern — `peek/src/components/InventoryHealthSummaryCards.tsx`
line 94** (commit `d41133f2`)
> ```tsx
> color: unhealthy > 0 ? "error.main" : "text.primary",
> ```
> 
> **Established pattern —
`peek/src/components/fleet/FleetOverviewTab.tsx` line 42**
> ```tsx
> color="warning.main"
> ```
> 
> ---
> 
> ## User impact
> 
> A user checking cluster health across pages will see "Unhealthy"
indices in red but "Unhealthy" Fleet agents in yellow — implying
different severity levels for the same concept, undermining the
color-as-severity language of the design system.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Suggested fix
> 
> Align on a single token for the "Unhealthy" status across both
surfaces. The Elasticsearch health model treats "red/unhealthy" as the
most severe state, so `error.main` is semantically correct.
`FleetOverviewTab.tsx` should be updated to use `color="error.main"` for
its "Unhealthy" card to match, unless the product team intentionally
treats fleet-agent unhealthy as a lower severity (in which case
`InventoryHealthSummaryCards` should downgrade to `warning.main`).
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Not a duplicate
> 
> Previous findings (#884, elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1744)
addressed Fleet staleness chip color and agent table status taxonomy
respectively — neither covers the cross-page mismatch between Fleet
overview stat cards and Indices/Data Streams stat cards for the same
"Unhealthy" label introduced by this new shared component.

commit.message:
builder: compute state root directly during block assembly (#19707)

## Summary

This PR contains two sets of changes:

### 1. Builder refactor: compute state root directly during block
assembly (#19318)

Remove the ExecV3 re-execution pass from `SpawnBuilderExecStage`.
Instead of building a block with `NoopWriter` and then re-executing via
ExecV3 to compute the state root, write state changes directly to
`SharedDomains` during block assembly and call `ComputeCommitment` at
the end.

**Key changes:**

- **`block_assembler.go`**: Added `SetStateWriter(w, advanceTxNum)`
method. When set, `ba.writer()` returns the real writer instead of
`NoopWriter`. `Initialize`, `AddTransactions`, and `AssembleBlock` all
use `ba.writer()`.

- **`exec.go`**: Uses `sd` (the parameter from `MiningStep`, already
backed by a `MemoryBatch`) for execution writes and `ComputeCommitment`.
A separate `filterSd` handles `filterBadTransactions` speculative
nonce/balance writes, which must not pollute the commitment.

- **Removed**: ~80 lines of ExecV3 orchestration,
`writeBlockForExecution` helper, `SendersCfg` parameter, parallel-tx
unwrapping logic.

- **`stages.go`**, **`backend.go`**, **`exec_module_tester.go`**:
Removed `SendersCfg` from `BuilderStages` call sites.

**Why filterSd is needed:** `filterBadTransactions` makes speculative
writes (nonce++, balance -= cost) to check transaction validity. If a tx
passes the filter but fails during EVM execution (e.g., gas limit
exceeded), `FinalizeTx` is never called, so the speculative write
remains. These stale writes would corrupt `ComputeCommitment` if they
went to `sd`. The separate `filterSd` isolates this speculation.

### 2. Varied BAL tests for Phase 1d/1e (#19632, #19633)

Extend the BAL (Block Access List, EIP-7928) test coverage beyond simple
ETH transfers to exercise diverse transaction types and
multi-transaction blocks:

- **Phase 1d (#19632)**: Individual tests for contract creation
(CodeChange), storage writes via token minting (StorageChange), and
multiple transaction types in the same block
- **Phase 1e (#19633)**: Execution-spec-test style scenarios with mixed
transaction types in a single block, including system transactions at
block boundaries

#### New Tests

commit.message:
Make addTagFilter idempotent to prevent duplicate tag filters (#2204)

Repeatedly clicking **Filter by** on the same span attribute in Traces
appends duplicate entries to `filters.tags`, producing duplicate filter
chips and bloated ES|QL with redundant predicates.

### Changes

- **`peek/src/store/useTracesStore.ts`** — `addTagFilter` now checks for
an existing `key/value/exclude` match before appending; returns a no-op
if the filter already exists
- **`peek/tests/unit/useTracesStore.test.ts`** — regression tests for
idempotency and for distinct combinations still being added separately

```ts
addTagFilter: (key, value, exclude = false) =>
  set((s) => {
    const exists = s.filters.tags.some(
      (t) => t.key === key && t.value === value && t.exclude === exclude,
    );
    if (exists) return {};
    return {
      filters: { ...s.filters, tags: [...s.filters.tags, { key, value, exclude }] },
      rawQuery: null,
    };
  }),
```

commit.message:
fix: codespace "New Session" button silently fails (#315)

## Summary

Fixes #314 — Clicking "➕ New Session" on a connected codespace group
(green dot) silently does nothing.

> **Note:** This PR depends on #308 (codespace integration). Merge #308
first, then this cleanly applies on top.

## Root Cause

Three independent bugs combine to create this silent failure:

| # | Bug | Impact |
|---|-----|--------|
| 1 | **Fire-and-forget async lambda** — `@onclick` handler is
void-returning, so Blazor never awaits `QuickCreateSessionForCodespace`
| Exception silently swallowed |
| 2 | **Error in wrong UI section** — `createError` only renders in
`CreateSessionForm` at top of sidebar, not codespace section | Error
invisible to user |
| 3 | **Stale client undetected** — Health check only checks
`tunnel.IsAlive` + `ContainsKey()`, never probes remote port | Green dot
stays after remote copilot dies |

## Changes

### `SessionSidebar.razor`
- Changed void lambda to `async () => { ... await
QuickCreateSessionForCodespace(...) }` so Blazor properly awaits and
re-renders
- Added `codespace-create-error` banner below codespace group sessions
(click to dismiss)

### `SessionSidebar.razor.css`
- Added `.codespace-create-error` style

### `CopilotService.Codespace.cs`
- Health check now does a TCP probe to the tunnel's local port when
tunnel + client + Connected all pass
- If probe fails: disposes stale client, removes from
`_codespaceClients`, falls through to reconnect path
- The green dot will correctly transition to reconnecting when the
remote copilot dies

### `CodespaceSessionCreationTests.cs` (new, 8 tests)
- Missing client → descriptive error (not "Service not initialized")
- No session state leaked on failure
- Non-codespace groups skip codespace guard
- TCP probe detects stale client (old code would skip)
- Healthy group still skips correctly
- All disconnected states block creation
- Connected state allows creation
- Error message mentions health check + retry guidance

## Test Results

2,223 pass (8 new), 3 pre-existing CLI binary failures (unrelated).

## Risk

- **TCP probe overhead**: One extra TCP connect per health check cycle
(15s) per connected codespace group. Sub-millisecond for localhost.
- **No behavior change for healthy connections**: Probe succeeds
instantly → `continue` as before.
- **Backward compatible**: No API changes.

Replace render-phase setState with useEffect in 10 locations (#2202)

Systematic refactor of the `const [prevX, setPrevX] = useState(x); if (x
!== prevX) { setPrevX(x); setY(...) }` anti-pattern, which forces a
guaranteed extra render/commit on every watched-value change.

### Before → After

```tsx
// before: setState during render triggers double render
const [prevTree, setPrevTree] = useState(tree);
if (prevTree !== tree) {
  setPrevTree(tree);
  setZoomPath([]);
}

// after: effect-based reset, single render
useEffect(() => {
  setZoomPath([]);
}, [tree]);
```

### Changed files

- **`usePanelData.ts`** — `prevSupportsImageExport` →
`useEffect([supportsImageExport])`
- **`ResizableSplitPane.tsx`** — `prevInitialFraction` →
`useEffect([initialTopFraction])`
- **`LogsSearchPanel.tsx`** — `prevSearchText` →
`useEffect([searchText])`
- **`ProfilingFlamegraph.tsx`** — `prevTree` → `useEffect([tree])`
- **`SimulateResults.tsx`** — `prevResult` →
`useEffect([simulateResult])`
- **`ParameterControl.tsx`** — `prevParamValue` →
`useEffect([currentValueInput])`
- **`useAddDataPageState.ts`** — `prevIngestCandidatesKey` →
`useEffect([ingestCandidatesKey])`
- **`QueryAnnotationOverlay.tsx`** — `prevFocused` →
`useEffect([editorFocused])`
- **`useRichIngestionVerification.ts`** — `connectionKey` state →
`useEffect([connection?.url])`
- **`useBatchedOverviewQueries.ts`** — `prevScopeRef` + render-time
setState → `useEffect([scopeKey])`

Net: **28 insertions, 52 deletions** — removes mirrored state variables
and their render-time comparison branches entirely.

*This section details on the original issue you should resolve*

<issue_title>[performance-sweeper] Render-phase state mirroring causes
avoidable double renders in 10 locations</issue_title>
<issue_description>## Findings

A single performance anti-pattern appears repeatedly across the React
codebase: **state is mirrored and reset during render** using the
pattern `const [prevX, setPrevX] = useState(x); if (x !== prevX) {
setPrevX(x); setY(...) }` (and equivalent key-comparison variants).

This forces a second render/commit whenever the watched value changes,
which adds avoidable work in already interactive surfaces (query panels,
logs, profiling, ingest, and dashboard hooks).

### Why this is a medium-severity quality issue

- It is systemic (10 verified locations).
- It introduces guaranteed extra render work on each source change.
- Several locations sit on high-frequency paths (query + visualization +
search flows).
- The same behavior can be preserved with a safer pattern: derive
directly in render when possible, or move reset logic into `useEffect`
keyed by the true dependency.

### Verified instances (exhaustive sweep for this anti-pattern family)

- [ ] `peek/src/hooks/usePanelData.ts:51-56`
- Evidence: `prevSupportsImageExport` + render-time `if
(supportsImageExport !== prevSupportsImageExport) { ...
setExportImage(null) }`
- Suggested fix: `useEffect(() => { if (!supportsImageExport)
setExportImage(null); }, [supportsImageExport])`

- [ ] `peek/src/components/ResizableSplitPane.tsx:33-37`
- Evidence: `prevInitialFraction` + render-time
`setTopFraction(initialTopFraction)`
- Suggested fix: move prop sync to `useEffect([initialTopFraction])` or
make pane fully controlled.

- [ ] `peek/src/components/logs/LogsSearchPanel.tsx:86-90`
- Evidence: `prevSearchText` + render-time `setGuidedInput(searchText)`
- Suggested fix: `useEffect(() => setGuidedInput(searchText),
[searchText])`.

- [ ]
`peek/src/components/visualizations/ProfilingFlamegraph.tsx:141-145`
- Evidence: `prevTree` + render-time `setZoomPath([])` when tree
changes.
  - Suggested fix: `useEffect(() => setZoomPath([]), [tree])`.

- [ ] `peek/src/components/ingest-pipelines/SimulateResults.tsx:18-22`
  - Evidence: `prevResult` + render-time `setExpandedDocs(new Set())`.
- Suggested fix: `useEffect(() => setExpandedDocs(new Set()),
[simulateResult])`.

- [ ] `peek/src/components/parameter-bar/ParameterControl.tsx:93-98`
- Evidence: `prevParamValue` + render-time `setDraftInput(...)` and
`setValidationError(null)`.
- Suggested fix: effect-based sync on `[param.value, currentValueInput]`
and remove `prevParamValue` state.

- [ ] `peek/src/components/addData/useAddDataPageState.ts:50,82-86`
- Evidence: `prevIngestCandidatesKey` + render-time resets
`setDerivedOtlpUrl(null)` / `setIngestAvailable(null)`.
- Suggested fix: `useEffect(() => { setDerivedOtlpUrl(null);
setIngestAvailable(null); }, [ingestCandidatesKey])`.

- [ ] `peek/src/components/QueryAnnotationOverlay.tsx:131,134-138`
- Evidence: `prevFocused` + render-time dismissal reset
`setDismissedForQuery(null)`.
- Suggested fix: `useEffect(() => { if (editorFocused)
setDismissedForQuery(null); }, [editorFocused])`.

- [ ] `peek/src/hooks/useRichIngestionVerification.ts:57-62`
- Evidence: `connectionKey` state mirrored from `connection?.url`, then
render-time resets `setPollingEnabled(false)` and `setBaseline(null)`.
- Suggested fix: move reset logic into `useEffect([connection?.url])`
(or derive key via ref + effect).

- [ ] `peek/src/hooks/useBatchedOverviewQueries.ts:93-97`
- Evidence: render-time scope change branch calls
`setKnownWithData(null)`.
  - Suggested fix: move cache invalidation into `useEffect([scopeKey])`.

## Suggested remediation approach

1. Add a lightweight team rule: avoid `setState` in component/hook
render bodies for prop/state synchronization.
2. Refactor all checklist items to either:
   - pure derivation in render (no mirrored state), or
   - `useEffect`-driven resets keyed to the source dependency.
3. For query-heavy surfaces, validate with React DevTools Profiler
before/after to confirm commit-count reduction.

## Duplicate check performed

Compared against `/tmp/previous-findings.json` from prior sweeper runs;
no existing finding matches this anti-pattern family.

fix: use deterministic controller-generated images for ImageMagick benchmarks

Previously im_4k.png was generated on each host with 'plasma:red-blue',
which produces a randomly seeded fractal on every run.  This contaminated
encode/decode timing comparisons: one host could receive a more compressible
plasma tile than another and finish faster without actually being faster.

New approach:
- scripts/generate_benchmark_images.py generates a 4096x4096 RGB PNG from
  a fixed random seed (default: 42) using numpy + Pillow, plus JPEG Q90
  and WebP Q90 derivatives.  The PNG is bit-identical across any machine
  and any re-run (md5 verified).  White noise maximises codec complexity
  and SIMD workload.
- roles/run_benchmarks/tasks/imagemagick.yml: replace the three local
  image-generation tasks with one 'delegate_to: localhost, run_once: true'
  task that runs the script if fixtures are missing, then ansible.builtin.copy
  to push all three files to each host.
- roles/run_benchmarks/defaults/main.yml: new run_benchmarks_fixture_dir
  variable (defaults to benchmarks/fixtures/ at collection root).
- benchmarks/fixtures/ is already covered by the benchmarks/ gitignore rule.

Usage: fixtures are generated automatically on the first benchmark run.
To regenerate manually:
  python3 scripts/generate_benchmark_images.py benchmarks/fixtures/ --force

commit.message:
Extract Fleet action loaders into `services/fleet/actions.ts` (#2212)

Begin decomposing the high-churn `fleet.ts` monolith (1307 lines churned
in 60 days) into domain-scoped modules under `services/fleet/`.

### Changes

- **New `peek/src/services/fleet/actions.ts`** — self-contained module
with `FleetAction`/`FleetActionResult` types, Zod schemas
(`actionSourceSchema`, `actionResultSourceSchema`), and loader functions
(`loadFleetActions`, `loadFleetActionResults`)
- **Slimmed `peek/src/services/fleet.ts`** — removed moved code,
delegates via re-exports:

```ts
export type { FleetAction, FleetActionResult } from "./fleet/actions";
export { loadFleetActions, loadFleetActionResults } from "./fleet/actions";
```

### Design notes

- `actions.ts` carries private copies of `parseFleetSchema` and
`readNestedString` to avoid circular imports (`fleet.ts` →
`fleet/actions.ts` → `fleet.ts`). Consolidating shared helpers into a
sibling module is the planned next slice.
- Zero consumer changes — all existing `import { … } from
"../services/fleet"` paths remain valid.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>


----

*This section details on the original issue you should resolve*

<issue_title>[refactor-opportunist] Modularize Fleet service by
extracting action loaders into domain module</issue_title>
<issue_description>## 🏗️ Refactor Proposal

**Summary:** Start decomposing the high-churn
`peek/src/services/fleet.ts` monolith into domain-scoped modules by
extracting Fleet action loading/types into
`peek/src/services/fleet/actions.ts` and re-exporting through the
existing API.

## Problem
`peek/src/services/fleet.ts` is currently a broad module that mixes many
Fleet concerns (schemas, types, fetchers, and UI-adjacent helpers),
which increases coupling and edit contention.

Concrete evidence:
- `peek/src/services/fleet.ts` has high recent churn: `commits=8
added=1079 deleted=228 churn=1307` over the last 60 days (`git log
--since='60 days ago' --numstat -- peek/src/services/fleet.ts`).
- One file previously contained Fleet action schemas + types + loaders
in addition to unrelated Fleet features (pre-change section around
former action block).
- Architecture guidance already prefers domain-specific service modules
for safer parallel development (`DEVELOPING.md` section “Adding a New
Elasticsearch Endpoint”, lines 119-139).

## Proposed Approach
Split Fleet service code into domain-scoped files under
`peek/src/services/fleet/` and keep consumers stable via re-exports from
`peek/src/services/fleet.ts` during migration.

What changes:
- Move Fleet actions-specific schemas/types/loaders into
`peek/src/services/fleet/actions.ts`.
- Keep compatibility by re-exporting those symbols from
`peek/src/services/fleet.ts`.

What stays the same:
- Existing import surface (`../services/fleet`) for call sites remains
valid.
- Runtime behavior and response parsing logic are unchanged.

## Proof of Concept
I partially implemented this refactor on one representative slice to
verify viability.

**Files changed:**
- `peek/src/services/fleet/actions.ts` (new)
- `peek/src/services/fleet.ts`

**Before → After:**

1) Fleet actions moved to dedicated module:
```ts
// peek/src/services/fleet/actions.ts:52-90
export async function loadFleetActions(client: ElasticsearchClient): Promise(FleetAction[]) { ... }
export async function loadFleetActionResults(client: ElasticsearchClient): Promise(FleetActionResult[]) { ... }
```

2) Monolith now delegates via re-exports:
```ts
// peek/src/services/fleet.ts:266-267
export type { FleetAction, FleetActionResult } from "./fleet/actions";
export { loadFleetActions, loadFleetActionResults } from "./fleet/actions";
```

3) Action-specific schema blocks were removed from `fleet.ts` (see diff
around former blocks near the top and end of file).

**Verification:**
- `make lint` ✅ passed (oxfmt + oxlint)
- `make test-unit` ✅ passed (`5` files, `70` tests)
- `make build` ✅ passed (Vite production build succeeded)
- No consumer import breakage observed because compatibility re-exports
are intact.

## Incremental Rollout Plan
This refactor can be completed incrementally:
1. **Done (proven):** Extract Fleet action types/loaders to
`services/fleet/actions.ts` with re-exports.
2. **Next slice:** Extract additional cohesive domains (`agents`,
`outputs`, `serverStatus`) into sibling modules under `services/fleet/`,
continue re-exporting from `fleet.ts`.
3. **Finish:** Move remaining shared internals (`parseFleetSchema`,
domain schemas) into targeted modules, then slim `fleet.ts` into a thin
compatibility barrel (or replace with `services/fleet/index.ts`).

## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Temporary import-path churn while splitting modules.
- **Mitigation:** Preserve stable public API via re-exports until
migration completion.
- **Risk:** Accidental behavior drift while moving parser/schema logic.
- **Mitigation:** Move code without semantic edits and validate each
slice with existing lint/test/build checks.

## Evidence
- Churn command output for `peek/src/services/fleet.ts`: `commits=8
added=1079 deleted=228 churn=1307`.
- Implementation evidence:
  - `peek/src/services/fleet/actions.ts:1-90`
  - `peek/src/services/fleet.ts:266-267`
- Duplicate checks:
- Reviewed `/tmp/previous-findings.json` (no matching open/active item
for this Fleet modularization).
- GitHub issue search returned none for this target
(`repo:elastic/ai-github-actions-playground is:issue (refactor fleet
service OR services/fleet.ts OR fleet modularize)`).

Smoke failure error message unconditionally references "PR comment" even
on `push` runs where no PR comment is posted. Eight scripts also use
"checkout" (git subcommand) instead of "check out" (verb phrase) in
user-facing CLI output.

logs/artifacts instead of a nonexistent PR comment:
  ```yaml
  if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
    echo "::error::…see PR comment for details."
  else
    echo "::error::…see workflow run logs/artifacts for details."
  fi
  ```

in the detached-HEAD guard across `run-detectors*.sh`,
`run-add-data-explorers.sh`, and `run-explorers.sh`

Extract shared `ToolbarRow` primitive for repeated flex-wrap control bars (#2206)

The same 4-property flex toolbar layout (`display: "flex"`, `flexWrap:
"wrap"`, `gap: 1`, `alignItems: "center"`) was copy-pasted across 17
files. This centralizes it into a single primitive.

### New component

`peek/src/components/ToolbarRow.tsx` — thin wrapper around MUI `Box`
with strict defaults and `sx` extension:

```tsx
<ToolbarRow>
  <DateRangePicker ... />
  <Button>Search</Button>
</ToolbarRow>

// Page-specific overrides via sx
<ToolbarRow sx={{ mb: 1 }}>...</ToolbarRow>
<ToolbarRow sx={{ justifyContent: "space-between", mb: 2 }}>...</ToolbarRow>
```

### Refactored call sites (17 total)

- **No extra styles:** DiscoverPage, HostDetailPage, HostsPage,
K8sDashboardControls, KubernetesPage, ServiceDashboardControls,
ServiceInventoryPage, IlmPage, IndexTemplatesToolbar, SnapshotsPage
- **Margin overrides:** DimensionOverviewGrid, MetricOverviewGrid,
ProfilingToolbar (`mb`), QueryPipelineSteps (`mt`)
- **Complex overrides:** ParameterBar (padding/border/bgcolor),
ProfilingFocusHeader (`justifyContent`), PageHeader (actions wrapper)
- Removed unused `Box` imports from 6 files where it was only used for
the toolbar pattern

### Tests

Unit tests added in `peek/tests/unit/ToolbarRow.test.tsx` covering a11y,
child rendering, container structure, and `sx` merging.

commit.message:
fix(script): validate --cpu, --memory, --disk-size format before calling terraform

Mirrors vm/variables.tf validation rules so the script fails fast with a
clear message instead of letting OpenTofu/Terraform catch it mid-apply:
  --cpu        must be a positive integer (>= 1)
  --memory     must match ^[0-9]+(Mi|Gi)$  e.g. 4Gi, 2048Mi
  --disk-size  must match ^[0-9]+(Mi|Gi)$  e.g. 40Gi, 100Gi

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: precompute column indices in parseSpansFromEsql hot path (#2150)

`parseSpansFromEsql` performed per-row `Map.get` lookups and
`isSpanLinkColumn`/`knownFields.has` filtering on every row.
`parseSpanEvents` called `.trim()` unconditionally on every string
input. On 10k-row benchmarks, trace events parsing median dropped from
~7.5ms to ~2.6ms.

### Changes

- **Precompute field indices** — resolve all `fieldMapping.*` → column
index lookups once before the row loop, replacing the per-row `get()`
closure
- **Precompute attribute columns** — filter known/events/link columns
once into `attrColumns` array, eliminating per-row `knownFields.has()`
and `isSpanLinkColumn()` checks
- **Skip link parsing when absent** — `hasLinkColumns` flag avoids
calling `parseSpanLinks()` when `links.trace.id`/`links.span.id` columns
don't exist
- **Preallocated array** — `new Array(values.length)` + indexed `for`
loop replaces `values.map()`
- **Fast-path trim in `parseSpanEvents`** — exact `"[]"` check before
trimming; charCode guard skips `.trim()` when no leading/trailing
whitespace
- **Missing timestamp coercion** — `startTimeUs` now falls back to `0`
instead of `NaN` when both timestamp fields are missing or invalid,
ensuring deterministic sort order for spans with no timestamp

```diff
-  const get = (row: unknown[], field: string): unknown => {
-    const idx = colIndex.get(field);
-    return idx !== undefined ? row[idx] : null;
-  };
-  return values.map((row) => {
+  const traceIdIdx = colIndex.get(fieldMapping.traceId);
+  // ... all indices precomputed once
+  const hasLinkColumns = colIndex.has("links.trace.id") && colIndex.has("links.span.id");
+  const spans: Span[] = new Array(values.length);
+  for (let rowIndex = 0; rowIndex < values.length; rowIndex++) {
+    const row = values[rowIndex]!;
```

feat: tags browser, video improvements, hero section layout (#43) (#45)

* Link section headers to tag browser with correct category

Each homepage section header now navigates to /tags?tag=<slug>:
- تغطية إخبارية → politics-and-economy
- سلسلة الوسائط → video
- أسلوب الحياة → social
- أرشيف → archive
- معرض حياة الشرق الأوسط → MEGallery
- بصري/كاريكاتير → caricature



* Fix spacing

* Fix video poster stretched on article page

Use aspect-video + object-cover for the poster image (was 1200x1500 portrait)
and wrap playing video in aspect-video container for consistent 16:9 ratio.



* Respect natural video aspect ratio (portrait/reel support)

Remove forced aspect-video — let the media render at its intrinsic dimensions.
max-h-[85vh] + w-auto constrains tall portrait videos without distorting them.



* Remove black margins when video is playing

Use w-full h-auto so video fills its natural aspect ratio with no letterboxing.



* Cap playing video height to screen height



* Fix hero section layout, responsive section headers, and env var cleanup

- HeroArticle: image fills remaining height (flex-1 min-h-0), responsive title font size
- ArticleCarousel: square aspect-ratio image, tighter spacing
- CompactArticleCard: smaller thumbnail, tighter title font
- SectionHeader: ResizeObserver shrinks font proportionally to prevent wrapping; kashida stretch recalculated at effective font size
- page.tsx: hero inner row flex-1 so badges align across hero/sidebar/carousel
- ArticleLightbox: use VideoPosterPlayer component for video playback
- Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix from GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_ANALYTICS, GOOGLE_TAG_MANAGER env vars



* Fix client-side env vars and lightbox index bounds

- Revert GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID to NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix so they are accessible in client components
- Fix ArticleLightbox: reset currentIndex when articles array changes to prevent out-of-bounds crash
- Update CMS.md env var documentation to match



* Update app/components/VideoPosterPlayer.tsx



* Update app/components/ArticleCard.tsx



* Fix video poster: fill container width and handle image load failure

- Button is now w-full so poster always occupies full container width
- Track posterError state; on img onError, fall back to the aspect-video no-poster button
- Previously: collapsed to ~24px when poster image 404d



* Revert "Fix video poster: fill container width and handle image load failure"

This reverts commit 49484350ac70a38dad8d5edc6aad6c8d8b422251.

* Revert "Revert "Fix video poster: fill container width and handle image load failure""

This reverts commit e4cdb3e31a7b2152f1b4894e13e1de79b0bdb09a.

* Center video in playing state



* Fix carousel badge alignment: push badges to column bottom with mt-auto

Carousel badges were stacking below the title with leftover space below,
causing misalignment with hero/sidebar badges. mt-auto pushes badges to
the bottom of the flex-1 carousel root, matching the hero article pattern.



* Hero Article Spacing Update

* Responsive fixes for md/iPad screens

- Header: add px-4 md:px-8 lg:px-12 to top bar to match navbar padding
- page.tsx: hero section breakpoint lg → md (desktop layout at 768px+)
- ArticleCarousel: h-52 on mobile, md:aspect-square on desktop



* Implement archive endpoint, show all tags, fix header padding

- Use dedicated /api/v1/archive endpoint for ?tag=archive with server-side
  sort (recent→latest, popular→most_read, featured→editors_pick)
- Add getArchive() to articleService with ArchiveItemSchema/ArchiveResponseSchema
- Show all contentType+tags on articles with fallback for unknown slugs
- Add missing tag aliases (video-article, laytaha, MEGallery, test)
- Add responsive horizontal padding to desktop header top bar



* Make lightbox responsive; add video articles to tag browser

- Lightbox mobile: natural image height, arrows overlaid on image,
  no empty space, scrollable info section
- Lightbox desktop: arrows outside card, 65vh fixed height, rounded card
- Tag browser: use /api/v1/videos endpoint for ?tag=video so articles
  include video.url and are playable in the lightbox



* Update homepage spacing and padding to match Figma design

- Add horizontal padding px-4 md:px-8 lg:px-12 to main container
- Increase section gaps from gap-6 to gap-8
- Adjust hero section divider top margin mt-4 → mt-6

Improve naming: add type aliases for interface{} parameters

- Add FramebufferHandle type in internal/render/present/ for platform-

- Add CustomEventPayload type in event.go for custom event data
  (3 usages updated)

while maintaining flexibility.
